title: Valid Tic-Tac-Toe State
pattern: Simulation â€” State Validity via Move-Count Invariants
coreIdea: Determine if a given tic-tac-toe board is a valid game state by checking logical invariants: move counts (X can have at most one more move than O), and that a player who has already won couldn't have taken further turns after winning.
recognitionCues: given a final/intermediate state, asks if it could have arisen from valid alternating play
not a search/traversal problem, but a validity-checking problem using counting and win-condition logic
approach: Count X's and O's on the board; valid only if count is equal or X has exactly one more (since X moves first). Check win conditions for both players. If O has won, counts must be equal (O just completed the winning move). If X has won, X count must be exactly one more than O. Both players cannot have simultaneously won.
timeComplexity: O(1) since board size is fixed (3x3)
